action: He returns from the east in darkness, soaked through, carrying a waterskin heavy with fresh water.
thought: She looked at me like I mattered.

action: He's finishing repairs to his house roof, using the last light before darkness falls completely.
thought: Shelter means safety. Shelter means I belong.

action: She sits by her hammock as night falls, fingers tracing the rope knots, thinking about thirst.
thought: Without water, we're actually finished, aren't we?

description: Marcus returns drenched with a lifesaving discovery—a freshwater spring—but his real admission, drawn out by Jade's pressure, is that he noticed her.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I was starting to worry. You've been gone all day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Went exploring. Found something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What? You look soaked.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water. Freshwater spring, east side, past the rocks.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're serious? We have water?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're welcome.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, I—sorry. That came out wrong. How did you even find it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Listened. Most things flow downhill. Took a while.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You did this alone. At night. For us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For you, specifically? No. For water, yes.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Don't do that. Don't push it away.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not pushing anything. I'm being honest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You could have told Ryan. Let him help.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I work better alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But you didn't have to.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You did.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Need it. Water. So I found it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There's something else you're not saying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe I just wanted to be useful.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're useful. You always are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Doesn't feel like it here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm just... not used to this. Being around people.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're doing fine.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't have to say that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not saying it to be nice. You found water when we were all panicking.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Ryan wasn't panicking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, but I was. And you noticed.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. I noticed.

action: He wakes before dawn and walks to the water source, moving through the semi-darkness with practiced ease.
thought: She said the water helped. That matters.

action: He emerges from his house as the light rises, stretching and already thinking about what he can build.
thought: I feel something good is building today.

action: She wakes in her hammock to sunlight filtering through the canopy, thinking of Marcus and how he brought her water.
thought: He brought water. I can't get past him.

description: All three head north together—Jade awake to Marcus's quiet knowing, Ryan energized by the work—to find higher ground for water collection.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Morning! Good to see the sun coming up properly.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water's good today.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, was just thinking about that. We're going to need a lot more if we want to—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Hi. Sorry, am I interrupting something?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No, no, of course not. Come on, fill up while it's cool.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's cold this morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It is. I was thinking... about something you said last night, Marcus.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That water's everything. You were right. I didn't sleep properly until I drank.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's good like that. Marcus knows what matters.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Just observation.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, I want to set up a better system for collection. Maybe a trap, or—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: A trap sounds smart. There was rain yesterday, so—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: North side. More runoff.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: North side, yeah okay. Marcus, you want to help me scout?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: After water.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I could help too, if you want.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The more the better. It's going to be a lot of work.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're quiet this morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Always quiet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He's reflective. I think that's good. Better than me running my mouth all the time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, I like it. It feels... honest.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Mm.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We should get going soon though. We're burning daylight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I'm ready.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Ready.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Great. Let's head north then. Jade, you'll see the valley I was talking about?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm excited to see what you're building.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: This is good. All three of us working together, it feels—

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Like we might actually make it?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: One day at a time.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: One day at a time. I'll take it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come on then.

action: Repairs the frame of his tent with branches gathered from the island, working methodically in the midday heat.
thought: She was at the water source with Ryan. Again. Not that I'm counting.

action: Approaches Jade at the water source with a warm greeting, already thinking of reasons to work beside her.
thought: She's genuinely happy to see me. That has to mean something, doesn't it?

action: Sits by the water collecting some for storage, her attention drifting between the work and the presence of both men.
thought: Everyone is building something. Building meaning, building shelter, building plans. I'm just here.

description: Ryan recognizes Jade's gift for listening and invites her to gather wood—and in her yes, the island's first real partnership takes shape.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey. You've been up long?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not really, just thinking. About all this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Thinking good or thinking hard?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Too early to tell. Maybe both.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fair. Listen, I was going to gather more wood before it gets too hot. Want to help?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why are you asking me?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why not? You're good company. And honestly, I like talking to you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. You actually listen. Most people just wait for their turn to talk.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that rare?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: More than it should be. Come on, there's a good spot near the clearing. Not far.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus mentioned a clearing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He did?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yesterday when we all talked. This is it, right?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, he's got good instincts for where things are. For people too, I think.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't know. He notices things. You can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You like him.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I do, actually. He's solid. The kind of person you want nearby when something goes wrong.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He brought us water. That mattered.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It did. More than solving a problem, I think. It showed something.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Showed what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That he cares. Even if he doesn't know how to say it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I can feel that in him. It's strange.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Strange how?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: A man like that, so closed off, but you can still feel the care. It's there underneath.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You really see him, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do I? I don't know. Maybe I'm just paying attention.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's seeing. Come on, let's grab some wood. I'll help with the bigger pieces.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. Lead the way.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We'll come back before it gets dark. That way everyone knows where we are.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Does that matter?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I think so. We're three people on an island. Right now, knowing where everyone is matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that about safety or something else?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe both.

action: Marcus reinforces his tent frame with salvaged driftwood, working methodically as the midday sun beats down.
thought: She keeps finding reasons to be near him.

action: Ryan hears a sound from the beach while gathering branches and hurries to find Judy collapsed on the sand, barely conscious.
thought: Someone's in real trouble. I can help.

action: Jade works on gathering coconuts when she hears Ryan's voice calling from the beach; she abandons her task and moves toward him.
thought: Something has changed. I feel it.

action: Judy washes up on the sand after hours of swimming, her body shutting down from exhaustion and dehydration until she hears voices approaching.
thought: Please let them be real. Please.

description: Judy emerges from the ocean—a plane-crash survivor who swam for hours while eight months pregnant—and Ryan and Jade welcome her as the island's fourth.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey—are you alright? Where did you come from?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I swam. The plane went down and I just... I couldn't stop swimming.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A plane crash? When was this? How long were you in the water?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't know. Hours and hours and I lost count of everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. You're safe now. What's your name?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Judy. Judy Chen. There was someone with me but the current was too strong and he didn't make it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm deeply sorry about that. But you did. You survived, Judy. That's extraordinary.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Ryan, who's this? Is everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Jade, this is Judy. She was in a plane crash offshore and managed to swim here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Judy, come sit down. You look exhausted. Here, take my hand.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry to just appear here like this. I'm so sorry.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Don't be sorry. You're here now. That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We found a fresh water source this morning. And we have shelter and food.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You're just... helping me? Both of you, without even knowing me?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course we are. Why would we not?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because it's who we are. Because you need help. That's the only reason we need.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I thought I was going to die out there. I really thought I wasn't going to make it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You didn't die, though. You're here and you're alive.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How far along are you? Is that okay to ask?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Eight months. Nearly there. The baby kept moving while I was swimming and that's what kept me going.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The baby is safe now. You both are.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: There's another person here too. Marcus. He's staying on the east side but he's capable and good. He'll want to help.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: So there are... four of us now? Four people?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yes. Four of us on this island. That's not nothing.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's get you to the firepit where it's warm and we can tend to you properly.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't want to just take. I need to contribute somehow. I need to pull my weight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: First, you rest. You eat, you drink, you sleep. Everything else comes after.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm alive. I really made it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You did. Come on.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll get you some water first, then food. Just small amounts at first, okay?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Yes. I can do that.

action: He gathered firewood as dusk fell, then positioned himself at the edge of the firepit, watching the group settle.
thought: I can't look away from this.

action: He brought Judy from the beach to the firepit, realized she had nowhere safe to sleep, and offered her his house without hesitation.
thought: She's trusting me with everything right now.

action: She watched the group gather as evening deepened and began helping gather what food and water they had to share with Judy.
thought: He's going to break his heart, I think.

action: She was brought from the beach to the firepit, and then Ryan offered her shelter in his house for the night—the first real roof over her head since the crash.
thought: Safe. Finally. The baby is safe.

description: Ryan leads Judy inside, Marcus keeps the fire burning, and Jade brings her water; the island's group completes itself—yesterday three, now four, survival bound together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy, come sit by the fire a minute. You look completely drained.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm okay. Really. I don't want to be any trouble.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not trouble. You're hungry and exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And you can't stay on the beach tonight. It gets cold, and you need shelter.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's right. You need a real place to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I've managed before. I can—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not tonight you can't. Look at yourself.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: My house has room. You're staying there. End of discussion.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: But where will you sleep?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Don't worry about that. My place is yours tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Exactly. We figure out the rest in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And I can help you get comfortable. We'll make it nice.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm eight months pregnant. I just swam to an island. I don't...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You did more than enough to survive. Now you rest.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: How long has it been since you've eaten?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't remember. Maybe this morning? I forgot after...

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay, we're feeding you first. Then sleep.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water too. You need water.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Let's go inside. I'll get you some fruit and coconut.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't want to take your things. You need them.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We have enough. Don't worry about that.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And you're not just our responsibility—you're one of us now. That's how this works.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: One of us?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. You're one of us. You got here. You're alive. You're in.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Come on. Stop arguing and let us help.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll get you settled, and tomorrow we figure out something more permanent.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Thank you. I don't know how to say thank you enough.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You don't need to say anything. Just get some rest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're one of four people now, on an island, who all need each other. That changes things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'll keep the fire going. You two get her inside.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And we'll make sure she's warm. That's what matters.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Will you be okay out here? It's cold.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I've got my tent. I'm fine.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on, Judy. Let's go.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We've got you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Tomorrow, when you've rested, you'll feel different. I promise.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. Yes. I can do this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You already did the hard part.

action: Returns to the firepit after dark and settles back, arms crossed, watching the stray and the others with careful eyes.
thought: What is it doing here? Why now?

action: Kneels gently before the stray, offering food and water with hands that shake slightly from emotion and determination.
thought: Something needs me. I can help it.

action: Sits near Ryan and the stray, speaking in soft tones, her expression softened by the firelight and empathy.
thought: It's like us—alone, terrified, needing home.

action: Watches the stray with intense protectiveness, tears streaming quietly, one hand resting on her belly, seeing her own survival in the lost puppy.
thought: We're both broken. We're both surviving somehow.

action: Creeps from the darkness of the beach toward the firelight, trembling and soaked, drawn by warmth and the desperate hope of salvation.
thought: Warm. Safe. Is it safe? Please.

description: A skeletal stray, trembling and desperate, eats from Ryan's hand, drinks his water, and curls beside the fire—Judy promising care, Jade claiming it as family, its terror finally giving way to sleep.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that... is that a dog at the edge of the fire?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [shivering, wet fur matted, creeping closer to the warmth]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Oh god. Look at it. It's so thin. So scared.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: It's just a puppy. Just a scared little puppy.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's starving. Look at its ribs. We have food, right?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whimpers, ears back, stopping ten feet away]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Let it come to us. Slow movements. It's terrified.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey, buddy. It's okay. You're safe here now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Oh sweetheart. Oh god, you poor thing. We'll take care of you.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [nose twitching, approaching Ryan's outstretched hand]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look at him. He's desperate for help but so afraid.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Here. Just some fish. Small pieces. That's okay, buddy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [eats tentatively from Ryan's palm, trembling, eyes wet]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Look at how hungry it is. How did it survive alone out there?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It didn't. It found us. That's what matters now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It needs water more than food. Let me get a bowl.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whines softly, tail moving slightly, watching Ryan leave]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: He's coming back. Stay with us. You're not alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're going to get you through this. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: [returns with water] Here. Drink, little one. Easy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [drinks deeply from the bowl, eyes closing, finally believing]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Can it stay? Can it sleep by the fire with us?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of course it stays. It's ours now. We're its family.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Tomorrow we'll build it a shelter. For now, by the fire is good.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [curls up slowly beside the fire, head dropping, safe at last]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look. It's actually sleeping. It trusts us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: We'll keep you safe. I promise. You're not alone anymore.

action: Wakes early in his tent and lies there for a long moment, his mind elsewhere, before rising to check the fire.
thought: I can't stop thinking about her hand.

action: Wakes beside Judy in the house, realizing he's slept deeply but still feels hollowed out; lies there gathering strength.
thought: How long can I keep this up?

action: Wakes in her hammock with unusual energy and heads into the jungle edge to gather firewood.
thought: The morning light feels like a gift.

action: Wakes in the safety of the house and sits up slowly, one hand on her belly, listening for sounds of the others.
thought: Another day. He didn't leave me.

action: Wakes under the palms where Ryan left it the night before, stretches uncertainly, and watches the beach path for movement.
thought: Will they come back? Am I safe?

description: Marcus tells Jade he came to disappear, but admits he doesn't know how to let anyone close—and when she simply sits with him, he realizes he needed exactly this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Since we put the puppy down for the night, you've been out here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's a good spot. Quiet.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You've been crying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: My eyes sweat when I stare at the horizon.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You, breaking. Just for a moment.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm fine, Jade.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Stop saying that. You're not fine and neither am I.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What do you want from me?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The truth. Why are you really out here?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I came to the island to disappear. Not like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not with people, you mean.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not with anyone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And now you're stuck with us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Now I'm stuck with you. That's different.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that bad?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I don't know. It scares me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because I don't know how to let anyone close.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're letting me close right now.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Jade...

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Just sit with me. You don't have to explain anything.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I used to think I was better alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Now I'm not sure what I want.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We have time to figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Do we?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yes. We're not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I think I needed this. You. This.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know.

action: He sits at the cliff lookout, throwing stones into the ocean one by one, each one an attempt to settle something restless.
thought: Tonight. What do I say tonight?

action: He sits near the firepit with the stray's head resting against his leg, slowly hand-feeding it scraps of dried fish, watching its shoulders relax.
thought: It's starting to trust me. That matters.

action: She approaches from the jungle path carrying an armful of coconuts and fresh palm fronds, moving with the ease of someone who belongs everywhere and nowhere.
thought: The light is perfect right now. Golden.

action: She emerges from the house door, one hand pressed to the small of her back, drawn by the sound of voices and the sight of Ryan tending something gentle.
thought: He feeds it so gently. Like he cares.

action: The puppy leans fully into Ryan's leg, its tail lifting slightly, testing whether the warmth and kindness are real or another false safety that might vanish.
thought: His hand is warm. Is this staying?

description: The stray finally falls asleep against Ryan, no longer afraid; Jade and Judy see in that moment what Ryan himself is slowly learning—that staying present is how you become real.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Here, slow. You're safe. Just take it easy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [soft whimper, sniffs the fish, takes it gently]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How much is it eating now? It looks less scared than yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: A little more each time. I think it's starting to understand we're not going to hurt it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Can I... do you want help? I could sit with it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah, you can. Just keep your movements slow. It spooks easy.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [ears perk up, glances at Judy but stays against Ryan's leg]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It bonded with you first because you weren't afraid. Animals know that stuff.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not sure what I was afraid of. Still am, kind of. Like if I stop moving, I'll just disappear.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You won't. You're here. You're real.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's why it trusts him. He actually shows up.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah well, showing up is the easy part. Staying—that's what matters, right?

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [tail wags once, then settles]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about what you said last night. About how something needs you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't remember saying that exactly, but... yeah. It matters to be needed.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Everyone here needs something. That's kind of why we're here, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe. Or maybe we're all just people who don't fit anywhere else, so the island fits us somehow.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Do you think we'll ever leave?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I think we'll stay as long as we need to. Then we'll know when to go.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [rests head fully on Ryan's thigh, a small sigh]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You know, Judy—the puppy came here because it was desperate, and it found the warmest place. That's what we're all doing.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I wasn't that brave. I didn't choose this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Neither did it. But you both survived. That's the same thing, kind of.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The island doesn't care how you got here. Just that you did.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [tail thumps against the ground, eyes drifting closed]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: It's falling asleep. It really does feel safe with you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're not safe. None of us are. But we're together. Maybe that's the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's sweet, Ryan. And probably true.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [soft snore, paws twitch in dream]

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I hope Marcus sees this. Maybe he'd understand why we're keeping it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus doesn't need to understand. He just needs to let us try.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He will. Marcus is harder on himself than anyone else. The rest of us he just watches.

action: Walks down from his tent just as Jade and Ryan settle by the fire, staying at the edge of the light.
thought: She picked him. Again. I watch from the edges.

action: Sits by the fire with the stray dozing against his knee, watching the embers glow steady and warm.
thought: I want to hold this moment. All of it.

action: Arrives with an armful of firewood, settles beside Ryan without hesitation, and feeds the fire a piece at a time.
thought: This feels right. Him, the fire, this peace.

action: Steps out onto the porch as the sun sets, watches the three of them by the fire from the shadows, then turns back inside.
thought: They don't need me there. They have each other.

action: Curls tight against Ryan's leg by the fire, its breathing deep and even, finally resting without flinching.
thought: Safe. He keeps me safe.

description: Marcus finally names the attachment between Ryan and Jade, kindled by firelight and shared purpose, as survival liability.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You came back. I wasn't sure you would after the firewood run.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The light was still beautiful. I couldn't miss it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: It's better now that you're here. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire won't tend itself. Someone should split the wood for tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I brought enough. It's stacked by the pit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I saw. Just saying, it runs low fast.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We can gather more tomorrow. Tonight I want to sit with this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: With what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: With knowing they're okay. Both of them.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup's okay because you fed it. That's not the same as everything being okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know that. But it matters.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It does matter. He's right.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Of course it matters. I just don't know how long we can afford to care.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's exactly when we can't afford not to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're not running out yet. Are we?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You haven't been tracking the stores.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then I will.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I will too. We can figure it out together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're by the fire every night now. Both of you. Together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're keeping the fire alive. That's the job.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You could join us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not built for sitting around.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're built for keeping watch. But you're not meant to be alone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I've been alone my whole life, Ryan. I'm fine with it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't think you are.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You don't know me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm starting to.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then you know I don't do this. I don't sit and talk about feelings.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: This isn't about feelings. It's about dinner. We're all tired.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Exactly. We should be splitting wood, not having a moment.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We can do both.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. You can't. One of you has to give.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm not stepping back, if that's what you're saying.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not asking you to. I'm just saying I see what's happening.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And what is that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That you're falling in. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And if we are?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Then at least one of us is still thinking clearly.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're jealous.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm practical.

action: He leaves his tent in the dark and walks to the firepit, unable to bear another night alone with his jealousy.
thought: I told him the truth. Now I don't know what to do with it.

action: He sits by the fire with the restless stray pup, offering quiet company as the night deepens.
thought: I want to understand what's broken between us.

action: She lies in her hammock, replaying the evening's moments and wondering what her closeness with Ryan has cost.
thought: Did I hurt someone I didn't mean to hurt?

action: She stands at the house door, listening to the low voices by the fire, watching the pup settle between the two men.
thought: They have each other. Where do I belong here?

action: It paces between Ryan and the fire, whining softly, until Marcus arrives and the two men find their quiet.
thought: The kind man's friend came. The air softens now.

description: When Marcus admits his fear, the stray—who had tensed and shifted away—cautiously edges back, settling between them as words soften toward reaching.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Can't sleep either.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No. The pup's been pacing. Seems anxious about something.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It picked the right person then.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What's that supposed to mean?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're good at making people feel like they matter. At being needed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is this about Jade? Because it's not like that. She was kind to me when I was falling apart.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm not accusing you. I'm just saying what I see.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But you're angry. I can feel it. What did I do wrong?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Nothing. You did nothing wrong. That's almost worse.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [whimpers, presses against Ryan's leg, sensing the sharp words between them]

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus, I care about you. That hasn't changed.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know. But you look at her differently than you look at me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's not true. You're just hard to read. I never know where I stand with you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I stand at the edges and I watch everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then why don't you just talk to me instead of suffering alone in the dark?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because talking doesn't change how things are.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [ears flatten, trembles, shifts away from the tension towards the fire]

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're shutting me out again, like you do with everyone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe I'm just protecting myself.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: From what? From me? I've never given you reason not to trust me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: No. You haven't. And that's the problem. You're too good, and I don't know how to be with that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then let me help you figure it out. You don't have to be alone in this.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [cautiously returns, sniffs Marcus's hand, tail tentative and hopeful]

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Maybe you're right. I'm sorry, Ryan.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Don't apologize. Just stay. Sit with us for a bit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I should go. It's late.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Please. Stay. The pup needs you to stop being afraid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's not the pup I'm afraid of.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. But maybe tonight you can let someone in.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [settles between them, still wary but willing, breath slowing to match the moment]

action: He wakes before the sun fully rises, restless from the night, and makes his way down to the firepit for water.
thought: He heard me break. I can't unknow that.

action: He wakes early in the house and finds the stray pup waiting by the garden gate; he offers it fresh water and the last of yesterday's fish.
thought: They both need me. I don't know if I'm enough.

action: She wakes uncertain, washes quickly by her hammock, then drifts toward the firepit where Marcus is collecting water.
thought: Did I hurt him? Does he hate me now?

action: She wakes alone in the house and stands in the doorway, watching the camp scatter to their separate rituals, watching them find each other.
thought: I don't fit anywhere. I don't know how.

action: It wakes under the palms with memory of warmth and pads toward the house, seeking the kind one who keeps fear at a distance.
thought: He's there. I'm safe if he's there.

description: At dawn, Jade makes Marcus name it plainly: something's there, and he admits—honest for once—he'll probably run.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Morning.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Hey.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You were here early.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Couldn't sleep.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because of last night?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about it. About you watching me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I wasn't hiding it well.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I liked that you watched. But I didn't like hurting you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You didn't mean to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Does that matter? Intent or impact?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Impact matters more.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then I hurt you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm sorry.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Don't be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Why shouldn't I be?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Because you didn't owe me anything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But I hurt you. That's on me.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're very determined to take the blame.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I prefer to face things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah, I've noticed.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that good or bad?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: For me, or for you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For both of us.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't want to hurt you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But I probably will again.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How do we do this, then?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We don't have to do anything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But something's there.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. Something's there.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Are you going to run away?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Probably.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: At least you're honest about it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's new.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What? Being honest?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Me being honest.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Ryan said you came to him last night.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He talk a lot?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He said you trusted him. That matters.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Yeah. It does.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm glad.

action: Emerges from the tent area toward the firepit, drawn by voices and laughter.
thought: She's laughing. That's not from before.

action: Tends the fire at midday, warm and present as Marcus and Jade arrive at the firepit.
thought: They're both here, both okay. That's enough.

action: Drifts to the firepit and settles beside Marcus, visibly relaxing as the morning's weight dissolves.
thought: He's okay. We're okay. I can feel it.

action: Sits at the beach edge, watching the distant firepit and trying to fill the void of time.
thought: They're together. I don't belong there.

action: Drifts closer to the firepit, drawn by the warmth and the ease in human voices.
thought: They're calm now. I can rest here.

description: When pressed on his closed-off tone, Marcus jokes—a rare crack—and the three choose to sit in silence together while the puppy draws close.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Hey, you're up. How's the tent treating you?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He says fine about everything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You do, actually. You feeling okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Why would I not be?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: After last night... and this morning...

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What about it?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Come on. You two had this whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I asked him if I hurt him, and—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You did.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: But you're fine now, right?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Obviously.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not fine. Your voice gets like that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Closed. You sound closed off.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not intentional.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. That's why I noticed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Well, we're here now. All three of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Is that what was supposed to happen?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't know what's supposed to happen.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe nothing dramatic. Maybe we just sit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sitting. How ambitious.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Did you just make a joke?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Apparently.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Should we document this? Marcus being funny?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better things to do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sitting. Not talking if possible.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's exactly what I'm doing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Then we're doing it together.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: The pup's come closer. He's less scared now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He can feel when things shift in us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're better. We're better.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We are.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: This is good.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: For now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'll take it.

action: Sits by the fire with the group, trading dry barbs with Ryan while watching Jade laugh.
thought: She laughs at my jokes. That's still surprising.

action: Settles by the fire with everyone gathered, warm and present, welcoming Judy into the circle.
thought: Everyone's here. This is what matters.

action: Laughs and teases between Marcus and Ryan, fully at ease, lighter than she's been in days.
thought: Both of them, easy together. It's good.

action: Steps out of the shadows and finally sits with the group at the fire, hesitant but present.
thought: They want me here. Actually want me.

action: Leans against Ryan by the fire, then walks slowly over to Judy and presses against her leg.
thought: She's safe. They're all safe.

description: Judy breaks her silence to ask if she belongs, and the answer comes not just in words but in the stray choosing her leg—a vote the whole group agrees with.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus, I've been thinking about your tent.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Don't.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: There's room in the house. You'd have actual walls.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Tent's fine.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He'll never leave that tent, Ryan. You know that by now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why not give it a try?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're so persistent about building things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's what happens when you can't let anything be.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I built us shelter. Real shelter. I'm proud of it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You did great. But maybe you love the building more than the houses.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He likes being in control.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: There's nothing wrong with wanting things solid.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Nothing. Except you can't stop.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He has a point though. You're always tense.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Someone has to be.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [wanders over and leans against Ryan's leg, seeking warmth]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look. Even the pup knows where safety is.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup is just hungry.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Aren't we all looking for something?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Some more than others.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Is it... can I sit with you all? I don't want to intrude.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy, yes. Come sit.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Please. We want you here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Sit.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I like being with you all. More than anything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You belong here. You know that?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You do.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: More than you think.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even when I'm just quiet and scared?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Especially then.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're not a burden.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're part of this. Of us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I don't always feel like I am.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then we're not saying it enough.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We're saying it now.

dialogue.speaker: stray
dialogue.line: [stands and walks to Judy, pressing against her leg with gentle trust]

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look. The pup is voting.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's his yes.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: He knows.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: This is good. All of us, like this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Really good.

action: He settles by the firepit left side, teasing Jade with a dry edge that surprises even himself; a real smile lingers after, unguarded.
thought: Laughing with her feels right now.

action: He welcomes Judy to the fire with genuine warmth, stroking the stray pup's ear while tending the flames—holding space for everyone, comfortable being the warmth.
thought: This is what it feels like to matter.

action: She sits at the fire, teasing Marcus while welcoming Judy—the bridge between all of them, easy and radiant, her own lightness generous.
thought: Everyone's here and safe. That matters.

action: She settles trembling at the fire, her hand finally steady enough to stroke the pup who chose her, tears that don't fall just behind her eyes.
thought: They wanted me here. They still do.

action: The pup curls against Judy's hip, sensing her fear and answering with quiet presence—safe now, trusted, the warmth of belonging at last.
thought: She's scared but safe. I'll stay here.

description: Three voices answer Judy's doubt: Ryan insists they want her, Marcus affirms she belongs, Jade invites her to sit—an unmistakable yes that she's one of them.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, for someone who claims to hate mornings, you sure wake up early to complain about them.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't hate mornings. I hate Ryan loudly making fire at dawn.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Every stick. Narrated individually.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's not fair. I'm methodical, not loud.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You announced each piece of kindling like it was a prize.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: With commentary.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You know what? Fine. But someone has to tend the fire, and clearly the two of you are content to sit in darkness.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're not content. We're just quiet.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even the pup understands my value.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The pup tolerates you. Barely.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Still learning who's safe.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah? Well, you both smiled today. I saw real smiles.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus smiled?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Not by choice.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That makes it matter more.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. I don't want to interrupt. I just... can I sit?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come here. Sit with us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yes. Please. We want you here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You do belong here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Oh... you want me too.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He knows who's good.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We all know.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Stop waiting for us to change our minds. We won't.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. I'm trying to believe that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You can. And we're here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We're staying.

action: Emerges from his tent as the sun breaks over the eastern ridge, stretching and pausing to listen to the camp stirring awake.
thought: She laughs at my jokes. Still surprising.

action: Crouches by the fire pit with fresh wood, watching the flames catch and strengthen in the early morning light.
thought: She should be awake soon. Wanting her to be.

action: Wakes in her hammock to golden light filtering through the palms, lies still for a moment listening to the camp, then pushes herself up.
thought: I want to know what I'm actually feeling.

action: Steps out onto the house porch into the early light, one hand on the wooden post, breathing in the salt air with something like peace.
thought: They chose me. They want me here.

action: Stretches from its sleeping spot in the shade near the palms, tail lifting as it scents the morning breeze and hears the camp waking.
thought: Ryan's here. Safe here. Everything safe.

description: Before the island fully wakes, Jade and Ryan speak what's been building: she's choosing him and staying, he's terrified of losing her, and both finally claim it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're up early.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Hammock gets too hot once the sun hits it. You sleep at all?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Not much. I was up thinking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: About what?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: About yesterday. About all of us actually fitting together like this.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We do fit now. That part changed.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. I felt lighter. Did you feel it too?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Something shifted, yeah. Everything felt different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I think it's because I stopped pretending I don't care about... about certain people.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Certain people. That's one way to put it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Specific people. You know who I mean.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I think I do. I've been thinking about you too, by the way.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. And about Marcus, but it's different. He makes me laugh—that's new.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That is new. He's been different since you got here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He has. But you were different from the start.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Different how?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You looked at me like I mattered. Like I wasn't just passing through.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You do matter. You're not passing through.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know that now. I was drifting before. Didn't know what I wanted.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Now I want to stay. Want to be here with all of you. Especially with you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That means a lot to me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know it does. That's why I'm saying it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was afraid you might leave.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not leaving. Not unless you want me to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't want that. I really don't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What are you thinking?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That I'm glad. That this is real. That you're choosing to be here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I am choosing. I'm choosing this. I'm choosing you.

action: Sits at the front of his tent, watching the firepit where Ryan and Jade sit close together, feeling the distance widen.
thought: She's choosing him. I already knew.

action: Sits by the fire at midday, drawing Jade close and listening as she admits what she fears most, his hand steady in hers.
thought: She needs me. She actually needs me.

action: Sits close to Ryan by the fire at midday, speaking her fear aloud for the first time and feeling his hand ground her.
thought: He's not leaving. He won't leave.

action: Gathers firewood from the pile at the camp's edge, working steadily and trying not to watch the three of them at the fire.
thought: They have something I need.

action: Rests against Ryan's leg by the fire, sensing the warmth between them and content to belong to this moment.
thought: Safe. All of us safe here.

description: Jade's promise to stay present—to hold his hand and stop thinking about leaving—finally stops her from running.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're quiet. Everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. Just thinking. About us. About this.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What about it? You're making me nervous.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: In a good way or bad way?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Depends on what you're about to tell me, I guess.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm not used to being wanted like this. Not like you want me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Is that a bad thing?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's scary. Because I might lose it. Lose you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not losing me. I'm right here.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But you could leave. Get bored. Find someone else.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I won't. I know myself. I don't fall halfway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's what scares me more. How much you mean it.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why does that scare you?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because if you leave, it will actually destroy me. Not like before.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: So don't think like that. Think like I'm staying.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I want to. I really do.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then do. Be here with me like you're choosing me too.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I am. I just said that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: No, you're here with me but you're still ready to run. I can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Maybe. That's how I've always survived.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not surviving anymore. You're living. With me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. And it feels real. That's the problem.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Real is good. Real is what I want.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Real means I could get hurt.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah. But it also means this matters. What we have right now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It does. More than I know what to do with.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then just hold my hand. Don't think about leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Promise?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Promise. Right now, I promise.

action: Stands at the tent edge as evening falls, watching the firelight glow in the distance where they sit together.
thought: She's choosing him. Still choosing him.

action: Tends the fire in the gathering darkness, the flames painting his face warm as he waits for her.
thought: She'll come back. She always does.

action: Walks slowly down from the path toward the fire, settling close beside Ryan as evening gathers.
thought: I can do this. I can stay.

action: Sits on the house porch in the growing darkness, watching the two figures by the fire with longing.
thought: They have something. They found something.

action: Settles between Ryan and Jade by the fire, belly warm, ears relaxed, completely at peace.
thought: Safe. Everyone safe here now.

description: Against her instinct to flee, Jade chooses to stay—not because she's suddenly brave, but because Ryan's unwavering presence makes running feel impossible.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're quiet tonight.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Just thinking. About you. About us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: What did you decide?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That I'm terrified. And that I want to stay anyway.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then stay. Please.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's not that simple.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Why isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because I'm the person who runs. What if I do that to you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You won't.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How can you be so sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because I know myself, and I know that if you're here, I'm not going anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Even when I'm hard to be around?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Especially then.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't want to hurt you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're not hurting me by being here. You could only hurt me by leaving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: But what if that's what happens?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then it happens. And I'll have loved you anyway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That seems reckless.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe it is. But I don't want to be careful with you anymore.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I don't know if I can give you what you need.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You already are. You're here. That's everything.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: How are you this sure?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Because I see you. The real you, not who you think you should be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That terrifies me more than anything.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I know. But you don't have to be scared alone now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You promise?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Every single day if you need me to.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if I wake up panicked?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then I'll be there.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay, I believe you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah. I'm choosing this. Choosing you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's all I needed to hear.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: For what?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: For being patient. For staying.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Always.

action: He approaches the fire to add wood as Ryan and Jade sit close; he kneels beside the flames and joins their conversation, speaking harder truths than he usually allows.
thought: She's beautiful when she's not afraid. He sees that too.

action: He sits at the fire with Jade beside him, their hands finding each other in the darkness, speaking softly through the night as the warmth settles between them.
thought: She's choosing me. She's really choosing me.

action: She drifts down to the fire as night deepens, settles against Ryan's side, and lets herself be seen by both him and Marcus at once—unguarded and real.
thought: I don't have to be afraid anymore. That's the real fear.

action: She stands on the house porch watching the fire below, seeing them gathered in the warmth; her hands grip the railing until her knuckles pale.
thought: I don't belong in that circle. I never will.

action: The pup presses close to the humans at the fire, its large head resting against Jade's leg, tail thumping steadily in the firelight as it feels all of them here, together.
thought: Safe. Everybody here. All safe.

description: Jade voices what she sees in both men—Marcus's careful kindness, Ryan's honest hope—and asks them to stay, choosing belief over fear.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You came back down. I wasn't sure you would.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Couldn't sleep. The house is too still when it's dark. Even knowing you're near, it's...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You're still scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not of you. Never of you. Of what it means that I'm not scared anymore.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then maybe it's time to stop being scared. I'll be here through all of it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire's burning low.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I was going to add wood in a minute. You okay?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine. I'll do it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're different. Less trapped-looking.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Is that... a compliment?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: It's an observation.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Marcus is secretly a poet. Compliments in the form of gentle insults.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's not poetry.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: No, he's right. You are like that. You say difficult things as kindly as you can.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Someone has to say true things. Everyone else just says what feels good.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I say true things.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You say hopeful things. Different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Maybe. But hope is true too, if you're willing to believe it.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I'm starting to believe it. That's the scary part.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Belief is always scary. It means you have something to lose.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That's the most philosophical thing I've heard you say.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I'm full of surprises.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're full of surprises but you never surprise me with meanness. I think that's why I trust you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You really are. I thought I had you figured out on the first day.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: And now?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Now I think you're the most careful person here. Like you're trying not to break anything—not the island, not the camp, not us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Hard to break things that are already broken.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Nothing here is broken, mate.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not broken either. Just... careful. I respect that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You should get some sleep. You look exhausted.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: So do you. But you'll stay awake anyway, watching the fire.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Someone has to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We could take shifts. I can keep an eye on it later.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Stay. Both of you. Just a little longer. Please.

action: He wakes to quiet tent light, gathers an armload of firewood, and makes his way toward the firepit.
thought: She's there. I can feel it.

action: He tends the coals of the firepit, adding wood with the care of someone who barely slept, watching the warmth build.
thought: She's here with me. This is real.

action: She drifts from the hammock toward the firepit, drawn by the light and the gathering, moving without hurry.
thought: Morning light and warmth. They're both here.

action: She stands on the house porch with a cup of water, watching the three at the fire in the distance, her hand moving to her belly.
thought: They have something I can't reach.

action: The pup pads soft-footed toward the gathered warmth of the firepit, tail cautious but rising, settling between the seated figures.
thought: Safe. All here. All warm.

description: They decide against splitting up: instead of separate hunts, all three pack supplies and move down the coast together, the pup trailing, intent on fishing the tide.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's still strong. Could've gone out with how hard we slept.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You kept checking it in the dark, didn't you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Can't help it. Just... habit.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fire doesn't keep itself.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Never said it did, mate.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should eat while we have the warmth. The food is running low.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Two days left if we're careful. We need to fish properly today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I can set traps down the coast. Need to move anyway.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: What if all three of us went together? The ocean's good if the tide is right.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: That work? All together?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Fine. Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He knows something's different.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: He knows we're okay. That's all he needs.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Marcus, wait—

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Nothing. Just... thank you. For staying. For being here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: I was here. Needed to be, actually.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We all were. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Water first. Then we pack for fishing.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I want to come with you. Both of you.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then let's move before the heat gets unbearable.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The fire's in good shape. We can leave it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Pup's ready. He wants to move.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We'll find enough out there. I know it.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We always do. When we're together.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Then let's go. All of us.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come on, then.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You coming, Marcus?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Right behind you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good.

action: Tends the fire with quiet focus, watching how easily Ryan and Jade fit together while the stray settles between them.
thought: I'm still here. That's enough.

action: Sits by the fire at midday with Jade beside him, absently petting the stray while Marcus brings firewood and joins the warmth.
thought: We have something real here. All of us.

action: Settles beside Ryan at the firepit, feeling the easy warmth of his presence and noticing how Marcus and the stray complete the circle.
thought: This is peace. Didn't expect it here.

action: Sits alone on the house porch, watching the four gathered at the firepit—Ryan, Jade, Marcus, and the stray—finding warmth in a circle she cannot reach.
thought: They have each other. I'm still outside.

action: Pads between the three at the firepit, settling against Ryan's warmth, then rises and trots toward the porch to check on Judy before returning to the group.
thought: Safe here. All safe. But... she's alone.

description: As the pup's faith in safety grows, Judy's distance becomes clear—and the three resolve to show her she belongs.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's holding steady. We're good for a few hours.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I love mornings like this—everything just... settled.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Better than yesterday's panic.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Look who's braver now. Having a good day, little one?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It's stronger. I can feel it—like it finally believes it's safe here.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Still flinches when the wind picks up.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's okay. So do I, sometimes.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: We're all learning, I guess.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're doing good with it. The dog, I mean.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah? I was just... there when it needed someone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: That's exactly what it needed. That's what we all need—to matter to someone.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy's been quiet today.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She's watching. Figuring out where she fits.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Do you think she's okay?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: I don't know. This morning she seemed... separate. Like she was already gone.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She is separate. But that doesn't mean she'll always be.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We should go to her. Show her she belongs with us.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You think she'd want that after this morning?

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: Won't know till you ask. But waiting sure isn't helping.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Look, even the dog knows.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: At least something's sure of the welcome.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You are too. You just haven't believed it yet.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Okay. Okay, yeah. After this—we'll go find her.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Good. Come on.

action: Returned from fishing and helped clean the catch by firelight, watching Jade draw Judy quietly into the group.
thought: She understood something we're all still learning.

action: Tended the fire for cooking while the pup and Jade worked to bring Judy back in without fuss.
thought: Maybe we broke something today. She's fixing it.

action: Left the fishing group's comfort to find Judy waiting alone and sat with her as evening deepened.
thought: She was drowning and I didn't see it.

action: Waited on the porch all day until the pup sensed it and Jade came, finally breaking the isolation.
thought: Maybe there's room. Maybe I'm not alone.

action: Left the fishing trio mid-evening and went straight to Judy, pressing close in the growing dusk.
thought: Her sadness called me. I went. That's how.

description: The pup, sensing Judy's loneliness, breaks from the fishing trio to find her on the porch, and Jade understands immediately.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You know we were fishing for dinner tomorrow too, right? Not just today.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I know. I guess I just... I'm tired all the time.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come sit with me by the fire?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You three were having a good day together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: We were. But the day's not over yet.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Ryan and you, you're so easy together.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's steady. But that doesn't mean...

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: And Marcus, he actually talked to you. He hardly talks to anyone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He talks to all of us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Not like that. Not with that... I don't know. Ease.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Come sit. Please.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: You feel bad for me.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I didn't know you were alone until now.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm always alone.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You're not. See?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: The pup just wants food.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: He's been eating fish all day. He wants you.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm scared.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Of what?

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: That there's no room for me here. That I'll always be watching from outside.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: There's room. Come sit with me. With us.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Why would you want that?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Because you're here. And because I didn't realize you were hurting.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I'm not angry at you.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: I know. But I'm still here now.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Fire's almost ready for dinner.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We got enough for days.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Judy's going to help us eat it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: If you want me to.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of course we want you.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: You're part of this.

action: He arrives at the porch as darkness settles, his simple presence completing the circle without need for words.
thought: The circle's whole. I'm part of it.

action: He settles beside Jade, making sure everyone is warm, his hand resting near hers on the bench.
thought: She's safe. We all are now.

action: She keeps herself close to Judy, her body angled toward her, one hand on her knee.
thought: I see her now. That changes everything.

action: She leans into the warmth around her, the stray's weight against her leg like an anchor.
thought: Maybe I don't have to be alone.

action: She presses against Judy's leg, sighing with the deep contentment of a pup who found her person.
thought: She's safe. The pack is whole.

description: On the porch as night falls, the group gathers—the stray pressed against Judy, who finally sits in the center, Jade beside her, Ryan and Marcus completing a circle that couldn't hold without her.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Everyone okay? Warm enough?

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Yeah, just sitting. It's nice.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I... can I stay? Out here with you?

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Of course you can. You always could.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: She knows that now, I think.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: The pup knows it. Come here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: She really came back to me. I wasn't sure if...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: She knows you're one of us. So do we.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The pup doesn't lie about these things.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Neither do we. Not tonight anyway.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: What changed? This morning I was...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: You were fine. It was us that was wrong.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: We saw it too late. But we're seeing it now.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: And we're not looking away again.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: I kept waiting for you to forget about me.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Judy, you're not the kind of person we forget.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: You matter. You're part of this.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: The circle doesn't work without you in it.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Even though I'm a lot? Even though I'm...

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Even because. You're exactly who we need.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Stop questioning it. Just be here.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Okay. I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: marcus
dialogue.line: That's all there is.

dialogue.speaker: ryan
dialogue.line: Should we think about heading in? It's getting late.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Not yet. Let's stay like this.

dialogue.speaker: judy
dialogue.line: Yeah. Like this. Please.

action: Wakes in the tent to grey-blue light and listens to the camp stirring without pulling away.
thought: The circle is real. I belong in it.

action: Feeds the fire with wood from the pile, moving slowly and humming something without thinking.
thought: Everyone's here. Everything feels right this morning.

action: Walks back from the beach with an armful of driftwood, barefoot and unhurried, noticing Marcus is awake.
thought: I'm not thinking. That's when I'm most honest.

action: Opens her eyes slowly in the house, checks that Ryan is still at the fire, that it's real.
thought: Maybe I belong here now. Maybe it stays.
